summary: Luxembourg's posture is set by the Ministry of State, whose national quantum technologies strategy charts a roadmap toward secure quantum networks and treats post-quantum cryptography as a quantum-resistant solution that can be integrated with existing systems. The government's digital-trust portal, run by ILNAS, has opened a public consultation on the European Union roadmap for the transition to post-quantum cryptography, which recommends that critical use cases migrate by the end of 2030 at the latest, and ILNAS has presented Luxembourg's view of the quantum threat and migration recommendations at the NISDUC conference. The National Cybersecurity Competence Center, NC3, has published a 2026 readiness survey produced with LuxQuantum that finds high awareness of the post-quantum threat across more than twenty-five organisations but weak practical action, and sets out five priorities including cryptographic inventory and crypto-agility ahead of the 2030 deadline.
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
migrationTimeline: 2030 | Critical and high-risk use cases migrated to post-quantum cryptography (EU deadline noted by ILNAS and NC3)
govActors:
Ministry of State | Luxembourg Ministry of State, issues the national quantum technologies strategy and roadmap
ILNAS | Luxembourg Institute for Standardisation, Accreditation, Safety and Quality of products and services, runs the digital-trust portal and the consultation on the EU post-quantum roadmap and presented at the NISDUC conference
Government of Luxembourg | publishes the national quantum communication roadmap factsheet (Phase 1 2025-2027, Phase 2 2027-2030, Phase 3)
NC3 (Luxembourg House of Cybersecurity) | National Cybersecurity Competence Center, published the 2026 PQC Readiness Survey Report assessing post-quantum maturity across Luxembourg organisations
mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (financial sector) | EU | binding-law
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on a coordinated PQC roadmap | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2026/2030/2035) | EU | soft-law
obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2, transposed into national law, and financial entities face equivalent duties under DORA; neither yet names post-quantum algorithms specifically. Migration follows the EU coordinated roadmap, with high-risk use cases targeted for 2030 and full migration by 2035, which is encouraged rather than mandated.
classificationBasis:
Ministry of State lead, strategy and roadmap, PQC as integrable quantum-resistant solution: Luxembourg's Quantum Technologies Strategy 2024 (ingested, T1)
ILNAS digital-trust portal consultation on the EU PQC roadmap, end-2030 recommendation for critical use cases: portail-qualite announcement 2025 (ingested, T1)
Phased roadmap (Phase 1 2025-2027, Phase 2 2027-2030, Phase 3): Quantum Communication in Luxembourg factsheet (ingested, T1); PQC reference not explicit in the snippet, flagged for review
migrationTimeline 2030 milestone is an EU roadmap recommendation that Luxembourg notes, not a Luxembourg national deadline - REVIEW
targetCompletion Phased: roadmap describes phases without a terminal date for the cryptographic transition
dominantProcess omitted: no standards camp (NIST/ETSI/ISO) named as national guidance in captured summaries
algorithms/standardFamilies omitted: not stated in captured summaries
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
